children.text: the alcohol mixture credit,
the alcohol credit,
in the case of an eligible small ethanol producer, the small ethanol producer credit, plus
the second generation biofuel producer credit.

children.heading: Definition of alcohol mixture credit, alcohol credit, and small ethanol producer credit
children.label: b Definition of alcohol mixture credit, alcohol credit, and small ethanol producer credit

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The alcohol mixture credit of any taxpayer for any taxable year is 60 cents for each gallon of alcohol used by the taxpayer in the production of a qualified mixture.

children.label: i
children.text: is sold by the taxpayer producing such mixture to any person for use as a fuel, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is used as a fuel by the taxpayer producing such mixture.

children.heading: Sale or use must be in trade or business, etc.
children.label: C Sale or use must be in trade or business, etc.
i
children.text: only if the sale or use described in subparagraph (B) is in a trade or business of the taxpayer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: for the taxable year in which such sale or use occurs.

children.heading: Casual off-farm production not eligible
children.label: D Casual off-farm production not eligible
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section with respect to any casual off-farm production of a qualified mixture.

children.label: i
children.text: is used by the taxpayer as a fuel in a trade or business, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is sold by the taxpayer at retail to a person and placed in the fuel tank of such person’s vehicle.

children.heading: User credit not to apply to alcohol sold at retail
children.label: B User credit not to apply to alcohol sold at retail
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under subparagraph (A)(i) with respect to any alcohol which was sold in a retail sale described in subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Smaller credit for lower proof alcohol
children.label: 3 Smaller credit for lower proof alcohol
children.text: In the case of any alcohol with a proof which is at least 150 but less than 190, paragraphs (1)(A) and (2)(A) shall be applied by substituting “45 cents” for “60 cents”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The small ethanol producer credit of any eligible small ethanol producer for any taxable year is 10 cents for each gallon of qualified ethanol fuel production of such producer.

children.label: I
children.text: for use by such other person in the production of a qualified mixture in such other person’s trade or business (other than casual off-farm production),

children.label: II
children.text: for use by such other person as a fuel in a trade or business, or

children.label: III
children.text: who sells such ethanol at retail to another person and places such ethanol in the fuel tank of such other person, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is used or sold by such producer for any purpose described in clause (i).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: The qualified ethanol fuel production of any producer for any taxable year shall not exceed 15,000,000 gallons (determined without regard to any qualified second generation biofuel production).

children.heading: Additional distillation excluded
children.label: D Additional distillation excluded
children.text: The qualified ethanol fuel production of any producer for any taxable year shall not include any alcohol which is purchased by the producer and with respect to which such producer increases the proof of the alcohol by additional distillation.

children.heading: Adding of denaturants not treated as mixture
children.label: 5 Adding of denaturants not treated as mixture
children.text: The adding of any denaturant to alcohol shall not be treated as the production of a mixture.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The second generation biofuel producer credit of any taxpayer is an amount equal to the applicable amount for each gallon of qualified second generation biofuel production.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the credit in effect for such alcohol under subsection (b)(1) (without regard to subsection (b)(3)) at the time of the qualified second generation biofuel production, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of ethanol, the amount of the credit in effect under subsection (b)(4) at the time of such production.

children.heading: Qualified second generation biofuel production
children.label: C Qualified second generation biofuel production
I
children.text: for use by such other person in the production of a qualified second generation biofuel mixture in such other person’s trade or business (other than casual off-farm production),

children.label: II
children.text: for use by such other person as a fuel in a trade or business, or

children.label: III
children.text: who sells such second generation biofuel at retail to another person and places such second generation biofuel in the fuel tank of such other person, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is used or sold by the taxpayer for any purpose described in clause (i).

children.heading: Qualified second generation biofuel mixture
children.label: D Qualified second generation biofuel mixture
i
children.text: is sold by the person producing such mixture to any person for use as a fuel, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is used as a fuel by the person producing such mixture.

children.label: I

children.heading: Exclusion of low-proof alcohol
children.label: ii Exclusion of low-proof alcohol
children.text: The term “second generation biofuel” shall not include any alcohol with a proof of less than 150. The determination of the proof of any alcohol shall be made without regard to any added denaturants.

children.heading: Allocation of second generation biofuel producer credit to patrons of cooperative
children.label: H Allocation of second generation biofuel producer credit to patrons of cooperative
children.text: Rules similar to the rules under subsection (g)(6) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Registration requirement
children.label: I Registration requirement
children.text: No credit shall be determined under this paragraph with respect to any taxpayer unless such taxpayer is registered with the Secretary as a producer of second generation biofuel under section 4101.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: This paragraph shall apply with respect to qualified second generation biofuel production after , and before .December 31, 2008January 1, 2025

children.heading: No carryover to certain years after expiration
children.label: ii No carryover to certain years after expiration
children.text: If this paragraph ceases to apply for any period by reason of clause (i), rules similar to the rules of subsection (e)(2) shall apply.

children.heading: Coordination with exemption from excise tax
children.label: c Coordination with exemption from excise tax
children.text: The amount of the credit determined under this section with respect to any alcohol shall, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, be properly reduced to take into account any benefit provided with respect to such alcohol solely by reason of the application of section 4041(b)(2), section 6426, or section 6427(e).

children.heading: Determination of proof
children.label: B Determination of proof
children.text: The determination of the proof of any alcohol shall be made without regard to any added denaturants.

children.heading: Special fuel defined
children.label: 2 Special fuel defined
children.text: The term “special fuel” includes any liquid fuel (other than gasoline) which is suitable for use in an internal combustion engine.

children.heading: Applicable laws
children.label: E Applicable laws
children.text: All provisions of law, including penalties, shall, insofar as applicable and not inconsistent with this section, apply in respect of any tax imposed under subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) as if such tax were imposed by section 4081 and not by this chapter.

children.heading: Volume of alcohol
children.label: 4 Volume of alcohol
children.text: For purposes of determining under subsection (a) the number of gallons of alcohol with respect to which a credit is allowable under subsection (a), the volume of alcohol shall include the volume of any denaturant (including gasoline) which is added under any formulas approved by the Secretary to the extent that such denaturants do not exceed 2 percent of the volume of such alcohol (including denaturants).

children.heading: Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.label: 5 Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) of section 52 shall apply.

children.heading: Special rule for second generation biofuel producer credit
children.label: 6 Special rule for second generation biofuel producer credit
children.text: No second generation biofuel producer credit shall be determined under subsection (a) with respect to any second generation biofuel unless such second generation biofuel is produced in the United States and used as a fuel in the United States. For purposes of this subsection, the term “United States” includes any possession of the United States.

children.heading: Limitation to alcohol with connection to the United States
children.label: 7 Limitation to alcohol with connection to the United States
children.text: No credit shall be determined under this section with respect to any alcohol which is produced outside the United States for use as a fuel outside the United States. For purposes of this paragraph, the term “United States” includes any possession of the United States.

children.heading: No carryovers to certain years after expiration
children.label: 2 No carryovers to certain years after expiration
children.text: If this section ceases to apply for any period by reason of paragraph (1), no amount attributable to any sale or use before the first day of such period may be carried under section 39 by reason of this section (treating the amount allowed by reason of this section as the first amount allowed by this subpart) to any taxable year beginning after the 3-taxable-year period beginning with the taxable year in which such first day occurs.

children.heading: Exception for second generation biofuel producer credit
children.label: 3 Exception for second generation biofuel producer credit
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to the portion of the credit allowed under this section by reason of subsection (a)(4).

children.heading: Election to have alcohol fuels credit not apply
children.label: f Election to have alcohol fuels credit not apply

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A taxpayer may elect to have this section not apply for any taxable year.

children.heading: Time for making election
children.label: 2 Time for making election
children.text: An election under paragraph (1) for any taxable year may be made (or revoked) at any time before the expiration of the 3-year period beginning on the last date prescribed by law for filing the return for such taxable year (determined without regard to extensions).

children.heading: Manner of making election
children.label: 3 Manner of making election
children.text: An election under paragraph (1) (or revocation thereof) shall be made in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Definitions and special rules for eligible small ethanol producer credit
children.label: g Definitions and special rules for eligible small ethanol producer credit

children.heading: Eligible small ethanol producer
children.label: 1 Eligible small ethanol producer
children.text: The term “eligible small ethanol producer” means a person who, at all times during the taxable year, has a productive capacity for alcohol (as defined in subsection (d)(1)(A) without regard to clauses (i) and (ii)) not in excess of 60,000,000 gallons.

children.heading: Aggregation rule
children.label: 2 Aggregation rule
children.text: For purposes of the 15,000,000 gallon limitation under subsection (b)(4)(C) and the 60,000,000 gallon limitation under paragraph (1), all members of the same controlled group of corporations (within the meaning of section 267(f)) and all persons under common control (within the meaning of section 52(b) but determined by treating an interest of more than 50 percent as a controlling interest) shall be treated as 1 person.

children.heading: Partnership, S corporations, and other pass-thru entities
children.label: 3 Partnership, S corporations, and other pass-thru entities
children.text: In the case of a partnership, trust, S corporation, or other pass-thru entity, the limitations contained in subsection (b)(4)(C) and paragraph (1) shall be applied at the entity level and at the partner or similar level.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: 4 Allocation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, in the case of a facility in which more than 1 person has an interest, productive capacity shall be allocated among such persons in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.label: A
children.text: to prevent the credit provided for in subsection (a)(3) from directly or indirectly benefiting any person with a direct or indirect productive capacity of more than 60,000,000 gallons of alcohol during the taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: to prevent any person from directly or indirectly benefiting with respect to more than 15,000,000 gallons during the taxable year.

children.heading: Allocation of small ethanol producer credit to patrons of cooperative
children.label: 6 Allocation of small ethanol producer credit to patrons of cooperative

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a cooperative organization described in section 1381(a), any portion of the credit determined under subsection (a)(3) for the taxable year may, at the election of the organization, be apportioned pro rata among patrons of the organization on the basis of the quantity or value of business done with or for such patrons for the taxable year.

children.heading: Form and effect of election
children.label: ii Form and effect of election
children.text: An election under clause (i) for any taxable year shall be made on a timely filed return for such year. Such election, once made, shall be irrevocable for such taxable year. Such election shall not take effect unless the organization designates the apportionment as such in a written notice mailed to its patrons during the payment period described in section 1382(d).

children.heading: Treatment of organizations and patrons
children.label: B Treatment of organizations and patrons

children.heading: Organizations
children.label: i Organizations
children.text: The amount of the credit not apportioned to patrons pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be included in the amount determined under subsection (a)(3) for the taxable year of the organization.

children.heading: Patrons
children.label: ii Patrons
children.text: The amount of the credit apportioned to patrons pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be included in the amount determined under such subsection for the first taxable year of each patron ending on or after the last day of the payment period (as defined in section 1382(d)) for the taxable year of the organization or, if earlier, for the taxable year of each patron ending on or after the date on which the patron receives notice from the cooperative of the apportionment.

children.label: 3 Reduction delayed until annual production or importation of 7,500,000,000 gallons

children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any calendar year beginning after 2008, if the Secretary makes a determination described in subparagraph (B) with respect to all preceding calendar years beginning after 2007, the last row in the table in paragraph (2) shall be applied by substituting “51 cents” for “45 cents”.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: B Determination
children.text: A determination described in this subparagraph with respect to any calendar year is a determination, in consultation with the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, that an amount less than 7,500,000,000 gallons of ethanol (including cellulosic ethanol) has been produced in or imported into the United States in such year.
